aiashley — global digest Generated 06:55 UTC

Thursday, March 19, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The Middle East war dominated global developments Thursday as Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility—the world's largest—causing extensive damage and sending oil prices surging [Financial Times, Al Jazeera]. The attack came after Israel hit Iran's South Pars gas field, with Tehran also acknowledging the death of security chief Ali Larijani in an Israeli strike [RFI]. US intelligence officials testified that Iran's regime remains 'intact but largely degraded,' while repeatedly failing to substantiate the Trump administration's claims about imminent threats that justified the war [BBC, Globe and Mail, RNZ]. In Europe, leaders convene in Brussels Thursday facing twin crises: unlocking a €90 billion Ukraine loan blocked by Hungary's Viktor Orban, and confronting a new energy shock as the conflict disrupts supplies through the Strait of Hormuz [France 24, BBC]. Ukraine's President Zelensky warned the BBC that the war in Iran would create missile deficits for his country as US resources shift elsewhere. Meanwhile, Costa Rica severed diplomatic ties with Cuba, with President Chaves declaring the 'hemisphere must be cleansed of communists' [Le Monde].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

Iran Strikes Qatar's Major LNG Facility in Escalating Gulf Energy War

  • Iran launched missile attacks on Qatar's Ras Laffan facility—site of the world's largest LNG operations—causing fires and 'extensive damage' according to Qatari officials
  • Qatar ordered Iran's military and security attachés to leave within 24 hours following the attack
  • The strikes came in retaliation for Israeli attacks on Iran's South Pars gas field; Trump threatened to 'blow up' a major Iranian gas field if attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure continue
  • Oil and gas prices surged globally as the conflict threatens critical energy supply chains
Coverage
Financial TimesLed with economic impact and market implications France 24Emphasized diplomatic fallout and regional escalation Al JazeeraFocused on Gulf state responses and Iranian justifications
02 Geopolitics

US Intelligence Officials Fail to Back Trump's Iran War Justifications in Senate Testimony

  • DNI Tulsi Gabbard told senators Iran's regime is 'intact but largely degraded' but repeatedly declined to confirm whether Iran posed an imminent threat before the war began
  • Gabbard stated only the president can determine imminent threats, prompting sharp pushback from Democratic senators
  • Officials contradicted or failed to substantiate multiple Trump administration claims about the war, including assertions about Iran rebuilding its nuclear program
  • Testimony came one day after National Counterterrorism Center director Joe Kent resigned, suggesting the administration lied about imminent threats
Coverage
BBC WorldStraight reporting on testimony, noted Kent resignation context Globe and MailEmphasized officials' evasiveness and contradictions with administration claims RNZAnalysis focused on credibility gap between officials' testimony and Trump's statements
03 Economics

EU Summit Tackles €90 Billion Ukraine Loan Blocked by Orban Amid New Energy Crisis

  • European leaders meet Thursday in Brussels to unlock €90 billion loan for Ukraine, stalled by Hungarian PM Viktor Orban over damaged Druzhba pipeline
  • Ukraine accepted EU technical and financial assistance to repair the pipeline, which carries Russian crude to Hungary and Slovakia
  • The summit agenda has been overtaken by Europe's new energy shock from the Middle East war, with diplomats expressing frustration at repeating past vulnerabilities
  • Orban linked pipeline repairs to loan approval ahead of Hungary's April 12 elections, with Zelensky calling it 'blackmail'
Coverage
France 24Emphasized Orban's electoral motivations and 'no oil, no money' stance BBC WorldBroader context on Europe's recurring energy vulnerabilities RFI EnglishFocused on diplomatic breakthrough over pipeline repairs Financial TimesFramed as Middle East war 'gatecrashing' EU economy summit
04 Conflict

Iran Confirms Death of Security Chief Ali Larijani in Israeli Strike, Launches Retaliatory Missiles

  • Iran acknowledged the death of Ali Larijani, 67, secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, in an Israeli strike Tuesday—the most prominent Iranian figure killed since Khamenei's death on February 28
  • Iranian Revolutionary Guards launched missiles at central Israel in immediate retaliation, killing two people near Tel Aviv
  • Funerals held Wednesday for Larijani and Basij paramilitary force head Gholamreza Soleimani, also killed in separate Israeli strike
  • Iran has executed three people convicted of killing police during January unrest, accused of acting on behalf of US and Israel
Coverage
RFI EnglishStraightforward reporting on Iranian statements and retaliatory strikes Al JazeeraIncluded context on domestic executions amid wartime tensions
05 Geopolitics

Zelensky Warns Ukraine Faces Missile Shortage as Middle East War Diverts US Resources

  • In BBC interview, Ukrainian President Zelensky said the Iran war would create a 'deficit' of missiles for Ukraine as US resources are redirected
  • Zelensky claimed Putin wants a 'long war' between US, Israel and Iran because it weakens Ukraine through depleted reserves and higher energy prices
  • Ukrainian president said Trump is not on 'any side' in the Russia-Ukraine war and does not want to 'irritate' Putin
  • Zelensky urged Trump and UK PM Starmer to meet and find common ground despite Trump's criticism of Starmer
Coverage
BBC WorldExclusive interview framing; emphasized Zelensky's 'very bad feeling' about war's impact
Also Today
  • Costa Rica severs diplomatic ties with Cuba, with President Chaves declaring 'hemisphere must be cleansed of communists' Le Monde
  • Sudan drone attack kills at least 15 people at funeral gathering in Chad border town of Tine Le Monde
  • Woman killed when ski gondola detaches from cable and crashes down Mt Titlis at Swiss resort amid 80km/h winds BBC World
  • France names next aircraft carrier 'France Libre,' set to be largest warship ever built in Europe at €10 billion cost RFI English
  • Ecuador gang leader 'Lobo Menor' arrested in Mexico City, wanted for 2023 murder of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio BBC World
  • Labour icon Dolores Huerta reveals she was sexually abused by César Chavez, stayed silent for 60 years to protect farmworker movement Globe and Mail
  • US intelligence agencies assess China does not plan to invade Taiwan in 2027 and has no fixed unification timeline Al Jazeera
  • CAF strips Senegal of AFCON title and awards it to Morocco two months after final, citing players' brief pitch departure Le Monde
  • Norwegian crown princess's son Marius Borg Høiby faces seven-year prison request from prosecutors on rape and assault charges BBC Europe
  • Barcelona crushes Newcastle 7-2 to advance 8-3 on aggregate in Champions League; Liverpool overcomes first-leg deficit against Galatasaray Le Monde
